channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Discarded bottles with liquids, 15th June
item.description:
Some bottles discarded with possible contents of oil or urine . Spotted them on Sunday 14th and they are still there . Some other rubbish close by too.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Grangehill Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley dumped - please collect, 15th June
item.description:
Please can this shopping trolley be returned to its rightful owner. It’s at the Rafford Way Entrance, at the side of the y buildings near the entrance to Bromley Palace Park/the old council chambers. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full litter bin again., 15th June
item.description:
Please can this litter bin be emptied as it’s full again, there is litter on top of the bin and around the bottom. Any chance of bigger bins?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin full again, 15th June
item.description:
Please can this bin be emptied. It’s over flowing and there is litter around the bin as people can’t put it in the litter bin due to it being full. Any chance of a bigger bin? Please??
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing ASB with young teenagers, 15th June
item.description:
Small group of teenagers congregating in The Ice House most late afternoons and evenings. Drinking underage. Smoking cannabis. Smashing vodka bottles (now on a daily basis), damaging fences, graffiti on The Ice House ceiling, climbing over the security fencing surrounding the ice house, damaging the wooden fencing around the Cascade and moat, throwing litter around (plastic bottles, cans, etc) and causing a general nuisance. We are arranging meetings with the police, but some of this falls within park security. Please can we have extra patrols. Many thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gazebo frame, cones, branches, 15th June
item.description:
Residents of Brindley close flats have created an obstruction to stop cars from parking at school drop off & pick up. No restrictions on parking in this road. Neighbours become abisive and intimidating towards female drivers. Telling us we cannot park on this public road!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brindley Close, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Outside 44 Sutcliffe rd spilt rubble

item.title: Smashed vodka bottle at the side of the ice house, 15th June
item.description:
Please can this smashed bottle be cleared up before someone or something gets hurt. Thanks. Ongoing problem.

item.title: Smashed vodka bottle in The Ice House, 15th June
item.description:
Please can this smashed glass bottle be removed from the ocean house before someone gets hurt or a dog or other animal cuts its paws. Thanks. Ongoing problem.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A teenager has been charged with murder after a 15-year-old boy was stabbed to death in east London. Police were called at about 19:15 BST on Monday to reports of a "large gathering, disorder and sightings of individuals in possession of machetes in Towpath Walk" in Hackney Wick. Officers and paramedics found Brayan David Saldarriaga, from Haringey, north London, with stab wounds in nearby Poplar Close, where he was pronounced dead. A 16-year-old, who cannot be named because of his age, was also charged with possession of a bladed article. He will appear at Thames Magistrates' Court on Saturday. Brayan's next-of-kin are being supported by specialist officers, the Metropolitan Police said. Anyone with information about the incident is asked to contact the force., 15th June
item.description:
Can Hackney Council please put some CCTV up working alongside metropolitan police since there has been an incident stepping of a young 15 year old boy. Residence do not feel safe. Authority you need to put safeguarding in CCTV, so much crime.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Poplar Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Irish traveller criminal car for crime run around. No policing in area, no enforcement, no CCTV to protect members of public from these criminal gangs. Hackney Council antisocial behavior department sit on their ass all day and do nothing about these problems in the area., 15th June
item.description:
Irish traveller criminals organized group. No insurance, no tax, not MOT, claimed number plates, stolen vehicles on daily basis, Your safer neighborhood police team for many years sit in the calf all day having lunch. Bloody marvellous.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Felstead Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bins don’t have a lid, foxes eat from bins every night, 15th June
item.description:
Repeat report after this system updated it as fixed when I did it mark as fixed, I simply updated my report!! Reported first via mobile in the Roads/highways category anonymously at 09:26, Sunday 7 June 2026 Sent to Royal Borough of Greenwich less than a minute later. FixMyStreet ref: 9573981. Photo of this report Photo of this report The large waste bins next to the flats on Hawkins Terrace are providing a major food source for local foxes. There is a small building immediately beside the bins which gives the foxes easy access. Every night they simply jump from the building into the bins, feed on the food waste, and by the following morning you can clearly see rubbish and food scraps scattered around where they have been searching through the waste. This appears to be a significant reason why there are so many foxes in the area and why they are causing ongoing problems for residents across the neighbourhood. Urgent action is needed to prevent access by installing suitable guarding around the adjacent building and ensuring the bins have secure, fox proof lids. Removing this reliable food source would help discourage the foxes from congregating in the area and reduce the nuisance they are causing. UPDATES I am a nearby resident and this is extremely problematic. The foxes are living in my back garden and every night return with food waste and rubbish. This is clearly exacerbated by the near by food source. Can you please address this matter as it’s extremely disruptive. My children are not comfortable spending time in the garden. Posted anonymously at 09:50, Sunday 7 June 2026 This is a really big problem for those living on Heathwood gardens whose gardens back onto Hawkins terrace. The foxes are living in the gardens now and this is dangerous for our small children and our pets. We are of course used to having foxes around here (as in all urban areas) but this is a completely different level and needs to be dealt with by the council. Posted anonymously at 10:15, Sunday 7 June 2026 I have noticed a definite change for the worse and covering up this waste would help. Foxes are rife now - breaking through fences and it’s a daily job to clean up fox poo. Posted anonymously at 10:21, Sunday 7 June 2026 Yes it has to be fixed. It’s not only about the foxes but it attracts fly tippers as well. Also Hawkins terrace looks awful so much rubbish strewn after high winds , foxes etc Posted anonymously at 11:03, Monday 8 June 2026 The foxes are a real nuisance. There’s fox poo all over our paths which is a danger to our grandchildren and the destruction in ours and our neighbours’ garden is extensive. They are clearly foraging from open bins and feel very comfortable in domestic settings. My cat chased one of them out of our conservatory. Please secure all open bins immediately Posted anonymously at 11:13, Monday 8 June 2026 I'm a nearby resident and the fox problem is getting worse and worse. They have destroyed by children's play equipment in our garden, leave faeces all over our front garden and terrorise our pet. This open food source is a clear reason why we all have so many issues with foxes here. Posted anonymously at 13:44, Monday 8 June 2026 Photo of this report This is a major health hazard, you can clearly see massively overfilling of the bins all around the bin area and a fox on top of the building about to start eating! Is this going to be resolved? State changed to: Fixed Posted anonymously at 22:55 today

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Little Heath, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rotten wood and rusted nails outside back of pub, 15th June
item.description:
This was left yesterday and today around Fenton Close by the tree at the rear of the Victoria pub in Fenton Close car park. There are also bags

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dalston Lane, Hackney, London

item.title: Lamp post defective - area dark, 15th June
item.description:
Lamp post 7 closest to Geffrye museum And Hoxton train station is defective not working. The area is dark and desolate. This makes it prone for ASB issues and risk for vulnerable people to use
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Geffrye Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
